title: Email not syncing
content: If your email client is not syncing new messages, first check your internet connection. Verify that your email account credentials are still valid by logging in via the web browser. In the email client settings, check that your IMAP or POP3 server address is correct. Try disabling and re-enabling the account. Clear the cache of your email application. Set the sync frequency to automatic instead of manual. If using two-factor authentication, verify that app-specific passwords are being used.

title: Monitor not detecting signal
content: When your monitor shows 'No Signal', first check that the cable is firmly connected at both the monitor and computer. If using HDMI, try a different HDMI port or cable. For DisplayPort, ensure the monitor supports the resolution you're sending. Restart your computer to force display detection. Update your graphics card drivers from the manufacturer's website. Try a different output port on your graphics card if available. In Display Settings, check if the monitor is recognized as a connected device.

title: Keyboard keys not responding
content: If specific keyboard keys are not working, check for physical debris or liquid damage first. Clean the affected keys using compressed air to remove dust. If using a wireless keyboard, check that batteries have charge and the wireless receiver is connected. Try unplugging and replugging the keyboard. On Windows, go to Settings and check Device Manager for any keyboard errors. Update keyboard drivers. If only one key is stuck, you may need to replace the keyboard.
